page_content:
Formerly: HESI217.

LEAD305 Leadership and Ethics (3 Credits)

Encourages students to explore the ethical demands of leadership. Through the examination of moral and ethical frameworks, students

will develop their efficacy in analyzing multiple perspectives, decision- making, and navigating complexity. This course focuses on values-based leadership and one's responsibilities and commitments in the context of leadership for the common good and for purposeful change. The course assists students in identifying and examining ethical leadership as it relates to values, authenticity, context, controversy, and dilemmas.
